title: 2018 AHA/ACC Guideline on the Management of Blood Cholesterol
organization: American Heart Association / American College of Cardiology
topic: blood cholesterol; dyslipidemia; statin therapy; ASCVD risk
summary: This guideline emphasizes heart-healthy lifestyle across the life course as the foundation of ASCVD risk reduction. It recommends statin therapy based on clinical ASCVD status, LDL-C level, diabetes, and estimated ASCVD risk, with shared clinician-patient risk discussion before primary-prevention statin therapy. It also recommends monitoring lipid response and adherence after lifestyle or drug therapy changes.
limitations: This is a US clinical practice guideline. It should be interpreted with patient-specific ASCVD risk, comorbidities, preferences, drug interactions, and local practice standards. It does not replace individualized medical judgment.

title: 2024 ESC Guidelines for the Management of Elevated Blood Pressure and Hypertension
topic: hypertension; elevated blood pressure; lifestyle; blood pressure lowering; cardiovascular risk
summary: This guideline defines hypertension as confirmed office systolic BP at or above 140 mmHg or diastolic BP at or above 90 mmHg, and introduces an elevated BP category. It encourages healthy lifestyle measures for all adults to prevent progression and reduce cardiovascular risk. Pharmacological treatment decisions depend on BP category, overall cardiovascular risk, tolerance, and patient context; treated systolic BP targets are individualized and require caution in frailty, advanced age, orthostatic symptoms, or limited life expectancy.
limitations: This is a European guideline and may differ from local or national hypertension recommendations. BP diagnosis and treatment targets require validated measurement, risk assessment, and clinician judgment.

title: WHO Guideline: Sodium Intake for Adults and Children
topic: sodium intake; salt reduction; blood pressure; cardiovascular disease prevention
summary: This WHO guideline recommends reducing sodium intake as a public health measure to lower blood pressure and reduce noncommunicable disease risk. WHO guidance for adults is less than 2000 mg sodium per day, equivalent to less than 5 g salt per day. Sodium reduction is relevant to hypertension prevention and cardiovascular risk reduction but should be interpreted within a full dietary and clinical context.
limitations: This guideline is intended for population and public health nutrition policy. Individual advice may differ for people with specific medical conditions, medications, or electrolyte disorders.
